Title: Rohingya man pleads guilty to attack leaving countryman partially paralysed
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: A Rohingya man was charged at the Sessions Court here today with causing grievous hurt to a countryman, resulting in the victim suffering paralysis on the right side of his body.
Mohd Rofik Syukur Mohd, 39, pleaded guilty after the charge was read to him before Judge Roslan Hamid.
According to the charge, Rofik is accused of intentionally causing grievous hurt to Shaiful Islam, 42, using a soil digger, an instrument which can cause death if used as a weapon.
He was charged with committing the offence at the roadside along Jalan Permatang Tinggi near IKS Pala Bukit Tengah, Seberang Prai Tengah, about 8.55am on May 4.
The charge was framed under Section 326 of the Penal Code, which provides for a maximum jail term of 20 years, and also whipping or a fine, upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Lim Zhan Yi prosecuted.
The court fixed tomorrow for facts and sentencing after taking into account the need for a Myanmar interpreter to ensure the accused fully understands the proceedings.
The court did not grant bail.
According to the facts of the case, the incident is believed to have stemmed from a dispute over food debt while the accused and the victim were living together as housemates.
As a result of the attack, the victim suffered head lacerations, brain haemorrhage, skull fractures and paralysis on the right side of his body.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bus driver critical, three passengers injured after bus slams into paving machine
Article:
BATU PAHAT: An express bus driver was critically injured after his vehicle slammed into a road paving machine at Km102.4 of the North-South Expressway northbound early today.
Three passengers also sustained injuries in the 12.57am accident, while 20 others escaped unharmed.
The bus, carrying 23 passengers, was travelling toward Kuala Lumpur when it collided with the roadworks machinery.
A team of 10 personnel from the Yong Peng Fire and Rescue station was deployed to the scene following an emergency call.
Operations commander Mohd Hafiz Othman said firefighters arrived at 1.05am and found the bus driver pinned in his seat.
"The rescue team used specialised hydraulic tools to extricate the trapped victim. The injured were rushed to a nearby hospital," he said in a statement today.
The rescue operation ended at 1.57am.
The New Straits Times has contacted the district police for further details. A statement expected to be released soon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Abd Halim Aman begins duty as new MACC chief commissioner
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman officially reported for duty today as the new chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC).
He takes over the agency's top post from Tan Sri Azam Baki, who officially retired after 42 years in the agency.
In a statement today, the MACC said Abd Halim's appointment was made through an appointment letter issued by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar on April 25.
The appointment received the consent of His Majesty the Yang di-Pertuan Agong in accordance with Subsection 5(1) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission Act 2009.
Abd Halim has been appointed on a two-year contract effective today until May 12, 2028.
All members of the MACC extend their congratulations and best wishes to him in leading efforts to strengthen integrity, governance, and the national anti-corruption agenda, the commission said.
Abd Halim replaces Azam, who ended his tenure yesterday after serving six years as
MACC chief commissioner
and more than four decades in public service.
On his final day yesterday, Azam urged MACC officers and staff to give their full support to the new leadership to ensure continuity in the agency's administration.
"Do not sabotage the new chief commissioner because if you do, it means you are sabotaging your own organisation," he said during a special appreciation ceremony.
"Please give your support to the MACC top management because they will continue our legacy. In any organisation, we must remain loyal to the leadership," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Australian real estate stocks rise as budget targets first-home buyer affordability
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Australian real estate stocks were set for their best session in nearly a week on Wednesday, led by gains in developers such as Mirvac and LendLease .
The rally reflects investor optimism that the government's 2026-2027 budget, which introduced fresh housing-support measures for first-home buyers, could redirect investor demand toward new builds, supporting housing supply and construction activity.
Here are some more details:
The budget proposes limiting negative gearing to newly built homes in a bid to support new housing supply.
Negative gearing lets property investors deduct rental losses from taxable income, a tax break the budget now aims to steer toward new-build homes.
NAB analysts see investor borrowing potentially becoming more closely linked to new housing construction because negative gearing remains available for investors in new builds.
Additionally, the government plans to scrap the 50 per cent capital gains tax (CGT) discount on assets held for more than a year from July 1, 2027.
Property investment firm Mirvac Group saw as much as a 3.9 per cent rise in its best intraday session since February 18, while LendLease recorded as much as a 4.6 per cent rise in its best intraday session since April 8.
Shares of other firms like Stockland Corp rose as much as 5.0 per cent in its best intraday session since August 20, 2025.
Housing-related stocks like Breville Group and GWA Group were up as much as 1.5 per cent and 3.2 per cent respectively. Breville designs small kitchen appliances, while GWA is a construction products supplier.
Analysts at NAB Economics and Markets Research say the reforms could bolster financial stability by curbing incentives for highly leveraged property investment, but may weigh modestly on house prices and add near-term pressure to rents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Why are Britons so fed up?
Article:
IN less than two years, Britain's governing Labour Party has gone from a landslide election victory to a historic rout at last week's local and regional votes that has put Prime Minister Keir Starmer's job on the line.
While incumbents across Europe face similar difficulties, Britain's politics have become ever more febrile in the decade since it voted to leave the European Union: Starmer, its sixth leader since then, promised change but has struggled to deliver.
While much of the voter anger appears to be directed at Starmer himself, opinion polls consistently point to a handful of core sources of frustration at both national and local level.
The gnawing sense of decline in current living standards and future prospects is felt by many households across the industrialised world.
In Britain that is sharpened by big earnings disparities between the wealthiest and the rest of society: OECD data show higher-than-average levels of income inequality and a big gap in the earnings of the top 10 per cent and those of the rest.
Moreover inflation has proven "stickier" in Britain than elsewhere - one reason why its interest rates are higher than in the euro zone, pushing up mortgage repayments for house owners.
A high exposure to gas prices also means Britain's economy is seen as being hit harder than others by the Iran war.
The tax take is also on course to reach a historic high, infuriating many voters, especially at a time when a record number of people are reliant on welfare benefits, feeding into an argument about fairness.
This is among the most polarising themes in domestic politics and sometimes beats the economy into second place as a top voter concern.
Controlling immigration was a top priority for pro-Brexit voters in the 2016 referendum but net arrivals reached record levels after it left the bloc - boosting the ratings of the anti-immigration Reform UK party of Brexit-backer Nigel Farage.
Since a 2023 peak, net immigration forecasts have been revised down, not least because more Britons are leaving the country.
Improving public services eroded by years of austerity after the global financial crisis of the late 2000s was a key election promise of Starmer in 2024 - and in particular to revitalise a state-run National Health Service stretched close to breaking point by the Covid-19 pandemic.
Waiting lists for healthcare hit a peak in 2023 and have since fallen back but they remain above pre-pandemic levels with lengthy backlogs for many services.
There is also widespread exasperation at under-resourced local government and justice services, plus patchy access to NHS dentistry.
Polling by the YouGov market research company ahead of the elections showed dissatisfaction with the state of Britain's roads ranked highest among local concerns, with 37 per cent saying it was the single most important issue facing their local area in a survey released last month.
There has been a huge media focus on the dangers caused by unrepaired potholes despite extra funds to help local authorities fix their roads.
One widely cited industry estimate in March said the backlog of repairs now meant only half the road network could be classified as in good condition.
Although headline statistics show a long-term drop in crime rates for most offences, surveys show one in five people consistently see crime and insecurity as the most important issue facing the country.
Analysts say some of that could be down to the way crime is portrayed in media or features in TV shows.
Others note genuine concern about the rise in specific offences like shoplifting, low conviction rates for other crimes and delays in justice due to lack of resources in the prosecution service.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mainland Chinese snap up Hong Kong homes in April, most in 2 years
Article:
HONG KONG: The number of Hong Kong homes bought by mainland Chinese surged 48 per cent in April from the previous month to a two-year high, data from property agent Midland Realty showed, supported by a strong yuan and a shift to purchasing from renting.
Mainland Chinese bought 1,892 homes in April, worth HK$18.9 billion. The combined transaction value was 31 per cent higher than in March and the most in 17 months.
Mainland Chinese accounted for 27.5 per cent of total housing purchases in April.
They favour new homes, with 1,032 of April purchases coming from the primary market and accounting for nearly half of the new home sales.
Midland analyst Benny Sham expects the trend will continue as more Chinese professionals working in the city decide to buy because rents are rising.
The home transaction volume and value last month were both the highest since April 2024, according to another realtor, Centaline.
Official data in March showed private home prices in Hong Kong rose for a 10th month in a row, helped by improved sentiment and pent-up demand.
Brokerage analysts forecast gains of at least 10 per cent for the full year, citing a resilient stock market, strong demand from mainland Chinese buyers and lower inventory.
Residential prices climbed 3.6 per cent in 2025, the first increase since they peaked in 2021. Since then, they have tumbled nearly 30 per cent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Public support for JS-SEZ hinges on jobs, housing and traffic solutions
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Public confidence will determine the long-term success of the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), according to a new study by consultancy firm Central Force International.
Central Force International is a Kuala Lumpur-based research firm specialising in public sentiment surveys, opinion polling and workforce studies across Malaysia and Singapore.
Its chief executive officer, See Toh Wai Yu, said economic transformation must be matched by visible improvements in daily life if the ambitious cross-border growth corridor is to sustain public support.
"Investment announcements may drive headlines, but everyday outcomes drive public confidence," he said, adding the confidence is not merely billions invested in the corridor.
The study, titled JS-SEZ Public Sentiment and Workforce Study, found that Johoreans broadly support the initiative, although backing remains conditional on whether the economic benefits are genuinely felt by local communities.
Johor recorded RM30.1 billion in approved investments in the first quarter of 2025, with about 90 per cent concentrated within the JS-SEZ footprint.
The investments are largely focused on artificial intelligence (AI), advanced manufacturing, digital services and medical technology sectors.
These industries are viewed as critical to Johor's transition into a high-value regional economy, See told the Business Times.
However, the study found residents were more concerned about practical issues such as jobs, housing affordability and traffic congestion than headline investment figures.
About 81.4 per cent of respondents identified job creation as the key factor influencing their support for JS-SEZ, followed by affordable housing at 70.1 per cent and better traffic planning at 47.3 per cent.
'See?' said the findings, showing that residents evaluate development based on whether it improves their everyday lives.
"Economic transformation succeeds when people can see where they fit within the future being built," he said.
The study also revealed that Johoreans favour development projects capable of generating employment and boosting economic activity.
Technology and innovation centres emerged as the top priority among respondents at 23.8 per cent, followed by industrial parks (18.6 per cent), tourism projects (18 per cent), housing and property developments (17.7 per cent), and commercial hubs (12.5 per cent).
District-level findings reflected differing local priorities.
Respondents in Johor Baru preferred technology centres and industrial parks, while tourism projects ranked highest in Kota Tinggi. Residents in Pontian and Tangkak placed greater emphasis on housing and commercial growth.
The report also highlighted Johor's growing workforce potential, with more than one-third of respondents holding university degrees and about 30 per cent completing upper secondary education.
Fields such as engineering, business, finance and information technology were identified as aligning closely with industries prioritised under JS-SEZ.
The study estimated that about 45 per cent of Johor's labour force could be categorised as "high-value talent", which refers to workers with advanced skills, technical expertise or specialised knowledge capable of supporting higher-income industries.
Another 25 per cent were deemed to have strong potential to move into higher-skilled roles through training and upskilling programmes.
However, the report cautioned that nearly one-fifth of workers could remain vulnerable as industries become increasingly technology-driven.
She said sustained investment in skills development would be crucial to ensure economic gains are distributed more broadly.
"Support for development is present, but it is conditional – and those conditions are clear," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Toddler critical after five-storey fall from JB PPR flat
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A toddler is fighting for his life after falling from the fifth floor of the Bandar Uda Utama People's Housing Project (PPR) flats here yesterday.
Johor Baru North police chief Assistant Commissioner Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said the boy, aged two years and 11 months, remains in critical condition following the 10.25am incident.
A witness, identified only as Tini, 40, said she was startled by the boy's mother screaming after discovering her son had fallen through a living room window at their Blok Teratai unit.
The victim fell onto the parking lot below and was found unconscious before being rushed to the hospital.
"As residents here, we sympathise with the family as they only moved in about a month ago. We do not know them well, but the boy lives there with his parents," she said.
Tini suggested the family might not have had time to install window grilles.
"The windows can be opened up to 90 degrees. Perhaps the family did not expect that it would provide enough space for such an accident to happen," she said.
Another neighbour, Aiman, 30, said the mother was traumatised after the fall.
"At first, I thought it was my child, but I checked and found all my children were safe at home. Shortly after, we heard the mother's screams," Aiman said.
"She looked devastated and was in shock. We hope he recovers fully and the family remains strong," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK's Savills expects reduced transactions due to Middle East war
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: British property advisor Savills said on Wednesday that it expects a fall and a delay in advisory transactions, weighed down by macroeconomic uncertainty arising from the Middle East conflict.
In its residential transactional advisory business, the firm has seen greater caution among both buyers and sellers since the onset of the US-Iran war, both in the war-torn region and the UK,  the company said.
Despite a slowdown in the Middle East, which contributed about 5.0 per cent to its 2025 underlying profit before tax, the firm retained its fiscal 2026 outlook.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three arrested for assaulting man at Jalan Besar Kepong restaurant
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Three men have been arrested for allegedly assaulting a man at a restaurant in Jalan Besar Kepong after accusing him of staring at them while they were eating.
Sentul police chief Assistant Commissioner Basri Sagoni said the investigation paper will be referred to the deputy public prosecutor soon for further instructions.
"The three suspects, aged between their 30s and 50s, have been remanded to assist in the investigation.
"One of the angles being investigated is whether the three men have links to secret societies," Basri said.
Basri said the case is being investigated under Section 324 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt using dangerous weapons or means, which carries a jail term of up to 10 years, or a fine, or whipping, or any two of the punishments upon conviction.
He added that the case is also being investigated under Section 427 of the Penal Code for committing mischief and causing damage, which carries a jail term of between one and five years, or a fine, or both, upon conviction.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/1437451/man-assaulted-allegedly-staring-drunk-kepong-restaurant-watch
It was reported that a man was assaulted by three others for allegedly "staring" at another man who was believed to be drunk, while eating at a restaurant in Jalan Besar Kepong.
The assault, seen in a viral video from closed-circuit television cameras posted recently, lasted several minutes.
The victim was sitting at a table in the restaurant when a man who appeared to be drunk walked up to him and assaulted him.
The culprit was soon joined by two others who at first tried to stop their friend, but joined in the attack.
The victim was punched, kicked and hit with various objects, including chairs and a kettle.
While the main suspect continued to assault the victim, his two friends later tried to calm him down, allowing the victim to flee.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pulau Pangkor migrant boat capsize: Three more bodies found
Article:
LUMUT:  Three more bodies of women, believed to be victims of a boat capsize incident involving undocumented Indonesian migrants off Pulau Pangkor on Monday, have been found.
Marine Police Region One Operations Command officer Deputy Superintendent Mazre Che Mahmod said the three bodies were discovered by Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) vessel KD Sri Indera Sakti about 20 nautical miles from the location of the incident at around 6pm yesterday.
"Investigations to determine the identities of the three victims are ongoing," he said when contacted today.
Mazre said the latest discovery brought the total number of victims found so far to 30, after 23 of them were rescued on the first day of the search and rescue (SAR) operation on May 11.
"We are still searching for seven more victims. The third day of the SAR operation resumed at 7am today, involving the Marine Police, RMN and the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA)," he said.
Earlier yesterday, four bodies were found in the waters off Pulau Pangkor.
Today's SAR operation involves two Marine Police boats, three RMN vessels and three MMEA assets — Maritime Vessel (KM) Malawali, BENTENG 1203 and a Bombardier CL415MP aircraft from the Subang Air Station.
Yesterday, MMEA Perak
rescued
23 Indonesian migrants after the boat carrying them capsized in the waters off Pulau Pangkor near Lumut on Monday.
The boat was reported to be carrying 37 undocumented migrants from Indonesia, who were believed to have departed from Kisaran, Indonesia, on May 9 for Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RTM to stop broadcasting channels on Astro from July 1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) will stop broadcasting services of its channels on Astro's satellite platform from July 1, after almost 30 years of collaboration.
In a circular dated May 11, RTM said both parties had found difficulties at this time to "reach a pricing agreement" and contact that meets their needs and financial capabilities.
"After many years of cooperation and continuous negotiations regarding the costs and rental rates for these services, it has become difficult at this time to reach a pricing agreement and contract that meet the needs and financial capabilities of both parties.
"Accordingly, RTM has decided to terminate the broadcasting services of RTM channels, namely TV1-HD, TV2-HD and Okey, on ASTRO's satellite platform and OTT platform operated by your party. This termination will take effect on July 1, 2026," it said.
RTM has collaborated with Astro in broadcasting TV1 and TV2 channels since 1996, and the Okey channel, formerly known as Muzik Aktif and TVi, since 2009.
The company added that its collaboration with Astro also included several joint venture productions and has had a positive impact on the audiences of both parties.
"As the channel rental costs continue to increase and RTM's financial burden in leasing these channels becomes heavier, it is more appropriate for us to terminate the service so as not to be burdened by costs that are difficult to control," it said.
Business Times has reached out to Astro for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Seberang Jaya assemblyman seeks probe into Penang foreign property pricing anomaly
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: An opposition lawmaker has called on the state government to conduct a detailed investigation into what he described as "significant anomalies" in foreign residential property ownership in Penang.
This follows official data disclosed during the first meeting of the fourth term of the 15th Penang state legislative assembly.
Izhar Shah Arif Shah (Bersatu - Seberang Jaya) said 2025 figures showed that nationals from mainland China owned 365 residential units in Penang with a combined declared value of RM150.2 million.
He said this translated into an average transaction price of RM411,506 per unit.
In contrast, he said Taiwanese expatriates in 2026 acquired 15 properties worth RM18.2 million, averaging about RM1.2 million per unit, which falls within the state's approved foreign ownership framework.
"The disparity in average pricing raises serious questions over compliance with Penang's foreign property ownership guidelines and minimum price thresholds," he said today.
Foreigners are allowed to buy property in Penang but must comply with strict regulations, including higher minimum purchase prices, state approval, and additional taxes.
Beginning this year, stamp duty on residential property transfers for foreign buyers rose to eight per cent, increasing overall acquisition costs.
Minimum price thresholds also remain significantly higher than those imposed on locals.
On Penang island, strata properties start from RM1 million and landed homes from RM3 million, while on the mainland, the minimum thresholds are RM500,000 for strata units and RM1 million for landed properties.
Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) participants may qualify for lower thresholds, generally between RM500,000 and RM600,000, depending on incentives.
All foreign purchases require Penang state authority consent under Section 433B of the National Land Code, along with a three per cent levy on the purchase price.
Foreigners are also prohibited from purchasing low-cost and affordable housing, as well as Malay Reserved Land.
Izhar questioned how transactions at such price levels were approved and whether weaknesses in the current regulatory framework could have been exploited.
"This average property price of RM411,506 is highly irregular and warrants immediate scrutiny.
"It is significantly below the minimum threshold set by the Penang government for foreign ownership," he said.
He stressed that lower- and middle-income housing segments must remain accessible to local residents, warning that any systemic loopholes could undermine housing affordability in the state.
"These housing categories are meant to put Penangites first," he added.
Izhar said the state government must clarify how such transactions were permitted and whether existing enforcement mechanisms are sufficient.
He urged the state government to initiate a comprehensive review and provide full transparency on the matter, including clarification from the relevant authorities on approval processes and compliance checks.
"We are calling for a thorough investigation and a clear explanation to the public.
"The integrity of our housing policy must be protected, and any loopholes must be addressed without delay," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three Chinese nationals linked to online scam deported to China
Article:
KOTA BARU: Three Chinese nationals linked to an online scam were deported to China on April 29 after all legal and documentation procedures were completed.
Kelantan Immigration Department director Garry Gambiri said the detainees were previously arrested for offences related to online fraud activities and were also wanted by Chinese authorities.
He said the transfer operation was carried out smoothly, systematically and under tight security without incident.
"The repatriation process was conducted after all legal matters and related documentation had been fully completed.
"It was carried out through close cooperation between the department, the Chinese Embassy and the Tanah Merah Immigration Depot," he said.
Gambiri said 14 officers from the Tanah Merah Immigration Depot, together with three enforcement officers from the state Immigration Department, escorted the detainees from the depot to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to ensure the operation proceeded safely and smoothly.
"Upon arrival at KLIA, 10 police officers from China took over escort duties and accompanied the detainees back to China.
Gambiri said the initiative was part of the department's ongoing efforts to strengthen detainee management and ensure compliance with established standard operating procedures.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gerakan: High fuel usage doesn't equal wealth in Budi95 review
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:  The government must not assume that motorists who consume more fuel are either wasteful or wealthy, Parti Gerakan Rakyat Malaysia (Gerakan) said.
Its secretary-general Wong Chia Zhen said the review of the Budi95 RON95 petrol subsidy mechanism must account for those who use more fuel due to work and family obligations.
He said this included long-distance commuters, delivery workers, and those who rely on a single vehicle to support an entire household.
Wong, who is also the Kulim assemblyman, said these realities could not be properly understood merely by studying figures and reports from an office.
"What the people fear is not just a reduction in subsidies, but the growing feeling that whenever there is a policy adjustment, those who are still struggling to survive are the first to be scrutinised and the first to feel the impact," he told the New Straits Times.
He also raised concern over the government's communication on the planned subsidy review, saying several senior leaders had given different explanations on the matter within 48 hours.
Wong said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had first said the government had agreed in principle to review RON95 subsidies involving the wealthy, with details to be finalised soon.
He said Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong then said the monthly RON95 quota could be reduced from 200 litres to 150 litres, before later clarifying that it was not a policy proposal and that his remarks had been taken out of context.
"After that, Transport Minister Anthony Loke came forward to explain that the cabinet had not made any decision on the subsidies and that the Prime Minister's earlier statement was not an official policy announcement.
"Within 48 hours, there were already three different versions of the explanation on Budi95.
"Is the government's communication becoming increasingly muddled, or is there a weakness in fiscal management that has led to this situation?" he said.
He said ministers and deputy ministers should avoid making "market-testing" statements on policies that had not been finalised or approved.
On Sunday, Anwar said the proposal for the rationalisation of fuel subsidies was being refined and was expected to be finalised soon.
He said the proposal was being reviewed by the Crisis Management Task Force under the National Economic Action Council (NEAC), chaired by Tan Sri Mohd Hassan Marican.
Economists have previously estimated that excluding the T20 group from the Budi95 subsidy programme could save the government up to RM1.5 billion a month.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's MY Value Up may spur investments: JP Morgan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's newly-launched MY Value Up programme could create investment opportunities for investors as companies focus more on improving shareholder returns and operational performance, JP Morgan said.
Malaysia announced the MY Value Up programme with the aim to shift corporate culture towards active value creation for shareholders.
The strategy targets companies above RM4 billion  (88 companies, 80 per cent market capitalisation), with a phased approach, initially voluntary but moving towards structured disclosure by 2027.
"Malaysia has recently taken a step up to jump on the bandwagon with the MY Value Up programme.
"While the initiative is still in its early phase, we believe investors can reap the opportunity to position in stocks that have the potential to unlock value," JP Morgan said in a note.
The US investment bank said the Securities Commission and Bursa Malaysia are undertaking early steps to drive reforms and broaden the visibility of Malaysian corporates.
The steps include launching the corporate scorecard, expanding the FBM KLCI index coverage from 30 to 50 constituents and promoting the Bursa Malaysia Quality 50 index.
"In the near term, there would be multiple workshops and discussions with corporate leaders, investors, and sell side firms to refine the program and develop tailored measures," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang wants cash, not projects, for forest reserve upkeep, says MB
Article:
KUANTAN: The Pahang government wants direct cash payments from the federal government for maintaining its forest reserves as water catchment areas instead of project-based compensation.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail said Pahang had received projects worth RM72 million since 2022, but no cash compensation.
"During a meeting, I requested that the federal government provide cash allocations instead of project-based compensation. If full cash payment is not possible, I suggested a 50-50 split between cash and projects.
"The Prime Minister (Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim)'s body language appeared agreeable, but to date, the compensation is still being provided in the form of projects. I will raise the matter again during the next National Finance Council meeting," he told the Pahang state legislative assembly during the question-and-answer session at Wisma Sri Pahang today.
Wan Rosdy was responding to a supplementary question from Datuk Andansura Rabu (Pas - Beserah) on whether compensation for the state's maintenance of forest reserves had been approved and, if so, the amount involved.
He said if the federal government continues to ignore the matter, the state executive council may one day consider using parts of the state's permanent forest reserve (PFR), which covers 57 per cent of Pahang, for economic activities to generate revenue.
Wan Rosdy said he had stated in 2021 that the state could generate about RM20 billion in gross revenue if it allowed logging and mineral extraction in permanent forest reserve areas.
"However, we also bear the responsibility of preserving water catchment areas that benefit not only Pahang but also other states through water supply," he said.
Earlier, in reply to a question from Mohd Zakhwan Ahmad Badarddin (Pas - Tahan), Wan Rosdy said Pahang's total PFR area covers about 1.589 million hectares, representing 44.13 per cent of the state's total land area of 3.60 million hectares.
He said the state's policies to balance development and environmental conservation include practising sustainable forest management and maintaining the current size of forest reserves.
"We are identifying other forested areas on state land through the Pahang Forestry Department to be proposed as new PFR areas. Last month, 524 hectares in Bera were gazetted as forest reserve.
"All PFR areas de-gazetted for economic development and public interest purposes must be replaced with other forested state land of equivalent value," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang pledges strict scrutiny for Bandar Gelugor project amid residents' protest
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has pledged strict scrutiny of all development proposals to balance public feedback, technical requirements, and sustainability following protests over a project in Bandar Gelugor.
State Local Government Committee chairman H'ng Mooi Lye said the state remains firm in its commitment to ensure orderly planning and full compliance with laws, guidelines, and sustainability principles.
"The state government, through the Penang Island City Council (MBPP), always takes into account all views and feedback received, including those of nearby residents, before making any decision.
"The state government remains committed to ensuring that every proposed development is carried out in a planned and sustainable manner, in compliance with all development principles, guidelines and applicable laws," he told the state legislative assembly sitting here today.
Elaborating, H'ng said all development applications would be assessed holistically to ensure they meet technical requirements, safeguard public interest and align with good governance principles and sustainable development objectives for the people of Penang.
His assurance comes as the proposed mixed development on part of Lot 642 in Bandar Gelugor here faces mounting scrutiny over traffic congestion, environmental considerations and infrastructure capacity, with concerns raised by elected representatives and residents.
Earlier today, a group of residents staged a peaceful protest outside the state legislative assembly sitting here, urging the state to halt the project.
Tens of thousands of residents from several neighbourhoods in Gelugor have voiced strong opposition to a proposed high-density mixed development on Lot 642 in Bandar Gelugor, located in the northeast district.
The
project
, proposed by I-Global Property Network Sdn Bhd, involves the construction of four residential apartment towers — two 45-storey blocks and two 61-storey blocks — along with office and commercial components on a 5.8-hectare parcel of government land formerly owned by Telekom Malaysia.
H'ng said the State Planning Committee had on Oct 30, 2025, ruled that the project must be referred to the National Physical Planning Council (MPFN) under Section 22(2A) of the Town and Country Planning Act 1976, in view of its potential high-impact implications.
He said the committee also required the submission of Traffic Impact Assessment (TIA) and Social Impact Assessment (SIA) reports as part of the evaluation process.
He said the developer's appeal for exemption, submitted through PLANMalaysia, was rejected by the State Planning Committee on Feb 20, reaffirming the requirement for MPFN consultation.
At the same time, H'ng noted that PLANMalaysia headquarters had taken a different position, stating that the development does not require MPFN referral as it does not constitute a new township, but instead involves a vertical residential project within the site.
On technical assessments, H'ng said the TIA has been submitted to the MBPP and is currently under review, while the Road Safety Audit (RSA) remains under the jurisdiction of the Public Works Department.
The SIA has yet to be submitted and will be evaluated by the state panel coordinated by PLANMalaysia Penang upon completion.
On environmental matters, he said the Department of Environment had determined that the project is not subject to mandatory Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) requirements under the Environmental Quality (Prescribed Activities) Order 2015.
He, however, stressed that compliance with the Environmental Quality Act 1974 remains mandatory in all circumstances.
The project has also drawn attention from several state assemblymen, including representatives from Sungai Dua, Seri Delima and Penanti, who raised concerns over traffic congestion, adequacy of infrastructure and the potential loss of green spaces affecting surrounding communities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia slips at midday on selective profit-taking
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended the morning session lower as investors locked in gains in selected plantation and utilities blue-chip stocks.
At 12.30 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 6.67 points, or 0.38 per cent, to 1,743.89 from Tuesday's close of 1,750.56.
The benchmark index, which opened 0.10 of-a-point higher at 1,750.66, moved between 1,742.45 and 1,752.64 during the session.
Market breadth was negative, with losers outnumbering gainers 529 to 421, while 545 counters were unchanged, 1,180 untraded, and 13 suspended.
Turnover stood at 2.39 billion units valued at RM1.44 billion.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said traders were awaiting the anticipated meeting between Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump in the coming days.
"We note market velocity is gathering traction as daily traded volume has surpassed the four billion level over the last two days. As such, we expect the index to trend within the 1,745–1,755 range today," he said.
Meanwhile, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd said heightened geopolitical tensions and a sharp rise in oil prices, amid the ongoing impasse between the United States and Iran, are likely to keep the KLCI in a range-bound consolidation phase.
"Investors will also be closely watching corporate guidance as they weigh still-resilient fundamentals against rising geopolitical risks, elevated energy prices and emerging growth headwinds," it said in a note.
Among the heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd fell four sen to RM11.20, Tenaga Nasional Bhd eased 14 sen to RM14.64, and IHH Healthcare Bhd slipped three sen to RM9.00, while Public Bank Bhd rose one sen to RM4.85 and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd added three sen to RM7.93.
Among the most active counters, Astro Malaysia Holdings Bhd and SMRT Holdings Bhd were flat at six sen and 27.5 sen respectively. WCT Holdings Bhd gained four sen to 52 sen, GIIB Holdings Bhd increased 3.5 sen to 24 sen and Genetec Technology Bhd edged up one sen to 37 sen.
Top gainers included UMS Integration Ltd, which rose 55 sen to RM8.55, UWC Bhd added 20 sen to RM5.93, Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd gained 18 sen to RM33.00 and Frontken Corporation Bhd was 12 sen firmer at RM4.69.
Among the top losers, Hong Leong Industries Bhd slid 30 sen to RM19.00, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd gave up 22 sen to RM29.68, BLD Plantation Bhd eased 20 sen to RM14.80, while Petronas Dagangan Bhd fell 18 sen to RM20.14.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index shed 36.82 points to 12,917.55, the FBM Top 100 Index weakened 39.19 points to 12,755.48, the FBM Emas Shariah Index fell 43.39 points to 12,830.90, the FBM Mid 70 Index lost 16.02 points to 18,517.05, and the FBM ACE Index slipped 4.85 points to 4,723.43.
By sector, the Financial Services Index erased 8.42 points to 20,292.88, while the Energy Index fell 4.91 points to 802.91. The Plantation Index declined 51.60 points to 8,746.46, while the Industrial Products and Services Index was up 0.29 of-a-point at 198.96.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former Bar chief urges protest over Jho Low pardon bid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysian Bar president Salim Bashir has urged Malaysia to protest to Washington over any move to pardon fugitive financier Low Taek Jho, a key figure in the multibillion-dollar 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal.
"The government, through the Attorney-General's Chambers, should write to the US Department of Justice raising concerns over the application and sending protest notes to the US government over any prospective move to consider Low's reprieve or pardon by the US president," he told the New Straits Times.
Salim said US presidential pardon powers should not undermine efforts to bring Low to justice over one of the world's biggest financial fraud scandals.
"The US pardon powers cannot be used to subvert the rule of law in bringing Low to justice for the mega financial fraud in world history," he said.
Salim made the remarks after reports emerged that Low was seeking clemency from US President Donald Trump.
Low, commonly known as Jho Low, faces corruption and money laundering charges in the US and Malaysia over his alleged role in the multibillion-dollar 1MDB scandal.
He has consistently denied wrongdoing through his legal representatives, claiming he is not a mastermind.
Salim said a US presidential pardon would not affect ongoing investigations or trials against Low in Malaysia.
"The US presidential pardon will not affect any ongoing investigations or trials against Low in Malaysia or other countries, and we cannot legally block a US pardon, but we should still express our objections and displeasure over Low's pardon application," he said.
The US Department of Justice website lists a pending request for a "Pardon after Completion of Sentence" under Taek Jho Low, filed in 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Spain pushes ahead with social media, AI rules despite Big Tech pressure
Article:
MADRID: Spain will push ahead with new rules to make social networks and AI safer despite intense lobbying from the tech industry, its digital transformation minister Oscar Lopez Agueda  told Reuters.
"The profit of four tech companies cannot come at the expense of the rights of millions," he said, adding that "powerful voices" were lobbying against proposed regulation that would curb high-risk AI systems or force companies to disclose how their social media algorithms work.
His comments echoed those by European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, who on Tuesday said the Commission was targeting addictive and harmful design practices by social media firms in its upcoming Digital Fairness Act.
Amid similar moves by Australia, France and Greece, Spain in February announced plans to ban social media use by teenagers - with a bill already working its way through parliament - and to adopt legislation holding executives personally responsible for hate speech on their platforms.
The move provoked sharp criticism from X platform owner Elon Musk, who called Socialist Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez a tyrant and a totalitarian.
Lopez said Spain wanted a common European approach as rules are easier to enforce across the bloc of more than 400 million citizens than country-by-country, and warned that backers of a laissez-faire approach would one day regret defending "the law of the jungle."
He linked the push to growing concern over cyberbullying, sexual harassment and AI-generated sexual deepfakes targeting children, especially girls, describing the impact on minors as a mental health pandemic.
Spain has positioned itself as one of Europe's most vocal advocates for what Lopez called "trustworthy AI," a model he said should protect privacy, democracy, minors and public safety rather than prioritise speed or profit.
Asked whether authorities should be able to identify people who use pseudonyms online if they commit crimes, Lopez said anonymity should not shield them from liability.
"What isn't legal in the real world cannot be legal in the virtual world. Full stop."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: From Compliance to Commitment: How Bursa Malaysia is shaping the ESG future
Article:
In recent years, environmental, social and governance (ESG) considerations have moved from the margins of corporate reporting to the centre of investment decision-making.
Around the world, stock exchanges are no longer seen merely as trading platforms but as powerful gatekeepers of sustainable economic behaviour.
In Malaysia, Bursa Malaysia has emerged as a key driver of this transformation, embedding ESG principles into its listing requirements, disclosure frameworks and market expectations.
As the nation charts its path towards sustainable and inclusive growth, Bursa Malaysia's evolving ESG agenda reflects a broader shift: from compliance-driven reporting to a more meaningful commitment to long-term value creation.
A Tiered Market with a Unified Purpose
Bursa Malaysia operates through three distinct markets - the Main Market, the ACE Market and the LEAP Market - each catering to companies at different stages of maturity.
While their entry requirements differ, a common thread now runs through all three: the growing expectation that businesses operate responsibly and transparently.
The Main Market, reserved for established corporations with strong profit records and substantial market capitalisation, imposes the most stringent governance and disclosure requirements.
These companies are expected not only to deliver financial performance but also to demonstrate sound risk management, ethical leadership and credible ESG strategies.
In contrast, the ACE Market offers flexibility to growth-oriented and innovative firms, particularly in emerging sectors such as technology and renewable energy.
While these companies may not yet meet profit thresholds, they are increasingly encouraged to embed ESG considerations early in their growth journey - an approach that aligns sustainability with innovation.
The LEAP Market, designed to support small and medium enterprises (SMEs), provides a lighter regulatory framework while still promoting basic transparency and accountability.
Alhough ESG requirements here are less onerous, the platform plays a vital role in cultivating responsible entrepreneurship, ensuring sustainability is not the preserve of large corporations alone.
Why ESG Matters to Bursa Malaysia
Bursa Malaysia's emphasis on ESG is not merely symbolic. It reflects a strategic response to global investor expectations, regulatory trends and economic realities.
Climate risks, social inequality and governance failures now pose material threats to long-term business performance. Ignoring them is no longer an option.
By introducing structured ESG guidelines and sustainability reporting requirements, the exchange seeks to enhance market resilience and investor confidence.
Transparency, after all, is the currency of trust in capital markets. Investors particularly institutional and international ones increasingly demand credible ESG data to assess risk, resilience and long-term value.
Importantly, Bursa Malaysia's ESG orientation also aligns with Malaysia's commitment to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
Through partnerships with regulators, industry bodies and civil society organisations, the exchange has positioned itself as a catalyst for green finance, responsible investment and sustainable corporate behaviour.
Raising the Bar on ESG Disclosure
One of the most significant steps taken by Bursa Malaysia is the introduction of mandatory sustainability reporting for listed companies.
Firms are encouraged to identify material ESG issues, set measurable targets and report progress transparently using recognised global frameworks such as the Global Reporting Initiative and the Sustainability Accounting Standards Board.
To reinforce accountability, Bursa Malaysia has also introduced an ESG disclosure scoring mechanism.
These scores, made available to investors, serve as benchmarks for comparing companies' sustainability efforts and reporting quality. In doing so, the exchange has transformed ESG disclosure from a box-ticking exercise into a competitive differentiator.
Yet challenges remain. While progress has been made, inconsistencies in reporting quality and depth persist.
Many companies still struggle with data collection, stakeholder engagement and translating ESG commitments into measurable outcomes. This highlights the importance of ongoing training, guidance and capacity-building initiatives led by the exchange.
Beyond Reporting: Measuring Real Impact
The true test of any ESG framework lies not in the volume of disclosures, but in tangible outcomes.
Encouragingly, evidence suggests that companies with robust ESG strategies are beginning to deliver measurable environmental and social benefits - from reduced carbon emissions to stronger community engagement.
Stakeholder engagement has emerged as a particularly critical factor. Companies that communicate openly with investors, employees and communities tend to receive stronger ESG evaluations and greater market trust.
This reinforces the idea that sustainability is not solely about policies and metrics, but about relationships and accountability.
Positioning Malaysia for a Sustainable Future
As ESG considerations become embedded in investment decision-making, Bursa Malaysia's role will only grow in significance.
By strengthening disclosure standards, promoting best practices and fostering a culture of sustainability, the exchange is helping to position Malaysia as a credible player in the regional and global sustainable finance landscape.
The transition, however, is ongoing.
Achieving meaningful ESG outcomes requires continuous learning, stronger enforcement and a shift in corporate mindset - from short-term profit maximisation to long-term value creation.
Ultimately, Bursa Malaysia's ESG journey signals an important truth: sustainable capital markets are not built overnight.
They are shaped through consistent policy direction, transparent governance and collective commitment.
In this respect, Malaysia's exchange is not merely responding to global trends - it is actively shaping the future of responsible business in the country.
*Dr. Hassan Fauzi is a professor at the Faculty of Accountancy Universiti Teknologi Mara in Puncak Alam, Selangor. Syed Iskandar Zulkarnain Sayd Idris is a senior lecturer at the Faculty of Accountancy Universiti Teknologi Mara in Puncak Alam.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Form One student dies after found unconscious in school hostel
Article:
GUA MUSANG: A 13-year-old male student died after he was reportedly found unconscious at the hostel of Sekolah Menengah Paloh in Gua Musang yesterday.
District police chief Superintendent Sik Choon Foo said police received a report at 8.53am yesterday from a man informing them that his son was unconscious at the hostel.
He said the school took the victim to the Chiku 3 Health Clinic for treatment.
"The medical officer at the clinic confirmed the victim had died. The body was taken to Gua Musang Hospital, and a post-mortem was conducted at 3.30pm to identify the cause of death," he said when contacted today.
Initial investigations revealed the cause of death was spontaneous bleeding in the head and pulmonary oedema.
Further examination found no elements of foul play.
Sik said the body has been released to the family for burial.
"The case has been classified as sudden death, and further investigations are ongoing."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CelcomDigi's Q1 earnings rise 8.85pct to RM418mil, revenue flat at RM3.21bil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: CelcomDigi Bhd's net profit grew 8.85 per cent in the first quarter ended Mar 31, 2026 compared to a year ago on the back of effective cost discipline.
The telco company posted a net profit of RM418 million, up from RM384 million in the same period last year.
CelcomDigi said it had established a more optimised cost base anchored on procurement and contract optimisation, as well as sustained site rental and network cost improvements, as it transitions from integration-driven synergies to structurally recurring operational efficiencies.
Its revenue marginally slipped to RM3.208 billion in the quarter under review from RM3.209 billion previously.
The company's service revenue grew 1.6 per cent year-on-year to RM2.7 billion supported by continued growth momentum in postpaid, home and fibre and enterprise solutions.
It closed the quarter with 20.4 million subscribers and declared a first interim dividend of 3.4 sen per share, in line with its sustainable dividend commitment to shareholders.
CelcomDigi chief executive officer Albern Murty said the first quarter's performance reflects the company's disciplined execution and the strong structural foundations it has built over the past three years.
"As we enter the next phase of growth, we are sharpening our portfolio to drive higher-quality, sustainable value for the consumer segment through stronger mobile and home and fibre propositions, as well as more personalised, segment-based services that enhance customer experience.
"We continue to focus on a more disciplined, data-driven approach to operational efficiency that will sustain growth and margin," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MK Land secures financial close for 29.99MW solar plant in Kedah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Property developer MK Land Holdings Bhd has achieved financial close for the development of a 29.99 megawatts alternating current (MWac) solar photovoltaic (PV) plant in Kulim, Kedah.
The project will be undertaken through the group's renewable energy subsidiary Citra Energies Sdn Bhd, under the government's Corporate Green Power Programme (CGPP)
MK Land said the financial close enables the full-scale rollout of construction works for the solar PV facility and a 132-kilovolt loop-in-loop-out substation, supporting efforts to expand Malaysia's renewable energy capacity.
The development is backed by a collaboration involving international and local stakeholders.
This includes global and financial institution specialising in sustainable finance, multinational corporate off takers and a local engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning service provider focused on renewable energy solutions.
Once operational, the solar plant will channel renewable energy into Tenaga Nasional Bhd's grid, with electricity supplied to corporate consumers through virtual power purchase agreements over a 21-year tenure.
MK Land chief operating officer of group resources and Citra Energies director Frankie Chai described the financial close as a defining step in its renewable energy journey.
"Beyond infrastructure, this project represents our belief in the future of clean energy as a driver of economic resilience and regional competitiveness," he said.
The latest project builds on MK Land's earlier renewable energy venture through Solar Citra's 10.95 megawatts large-scale solar PV plan in Lembah Beriah, Perak, developed under Malaysia's large-scale solar 4 (LSS4) programme.
Aligned with Malaysia's National Energy Transition Roadmap, the Citra Energies project reinforces MK Land's continued commitment to supporting the nation's long-term net-zero commitments towards 2050.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former bank sales head fined RM90,000 for bribery, failure to report corrupt payments
Article:
MELAKA: A former bank employee was fined a total of RM90,000 by the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court today after pleading guilty to 17 charges of bribery and failing to report corrupt payments.
The charges were linked to housing loan referrals made during his tenure as a regional sales head.
Judge Elesabet Paya Wan ordered the accused, Mohd Rais Mohamed Ramli, 44, to serve between two and 10 months' jail for each charge if he fails to pay the fine.
According to the charges, Mohd Rais accepted bribes totalling RM123,132.50 from a law firm through his wife's company account.
The payments, ranging from RM2,820 to RM15,545, were an inducement to help the law firm secure housing loan case referrals through a bank executive officer under his supervision.
The offences were committed at a bank branch in Taman Seri Gombak, Batu Caves, Selangor, between Dec 13, 2019, and Jan 27, 2023.
He was charged under Section 16(a)(A) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009 for accepting bribes and Section 25(1) of the same Act for failing to report the corrupt offers.
In mitigation, defence counsel S.S. Amritpal Singh said his client is currently unemployed and has a wife and three children to support.
MACC deputy public prosecutor Sharina Farhana Nor Sa'ari appeared for the prosecution.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippine senator seeks military support to block ICC drug war arrest
Article:
MANILA: Former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte's chief drug war enforcer urged the military on Wednesday to stop government attempts to arrest and fly him to the Netherlands to stand trial on charges of crimes against humanity.
Ronald Dela Rosa, a sitting senator and former police chief, began his third day holed up at the Senate building after its leadership stopped government efforts to serve an arrest warrant from the International Criminal Court over his role in Duterte's bloody drug war.
Dela Rosa, better known by his nickname "Bato", is accused of the crime against humanity of murder along with Duterte and other co-perpetrators.
"I am not appealing for violent support. I am appealing for peaceful support," Dela Rosa told reporters.
He urged "my fellow men in uniform" and former classmates at the Philippine Military Academy, which produces most of the armed forces' officer corps, to "express their sentiment" that President Ferdinand Marcos's government "should not hand me over to foreigners."
Outside the Senate on Wednesday, about 500 riot police faced off with some 250 protesters demanding the arrest and handover to the ICC of a person they described as the "architect" of Duterte's drug war.
The crackdown left thousands dead, human rights monitors say, many of them drug users and low-level narcotics peddlers.
Dela Rosa was police chief in 2016-2018, during Duterte's first two years in office.
Duterte was arrested in March last year, flown to the Netherlands on the same day, and is detained in the Hague where he awaits trial.
The senator had not been seen publicly since November before emerging on Monday to take part in an unexpected vote that helped Duterte loyalists capture control of the Senate.
The new senate leadership said it would only allow Dela Rosa's arrest if it was ordered by a Philippine court.
A Marcos spokeswoman said Tuesday the president would "not interfere in the decisions of the Senate."
The Supreme Court has yet to act on a Dela Rosa petition to stop the Manila government from enforcing the ICC arrest warrant.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang Pas to lodge MACC report over alleged zakat fund misuse, governance issues
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang Pas will lodge a report with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today over alleged governance irregularities and the handling of zakat funds involving the Penang Islamic religious authorities.
Pas Youth deputy chief Muhammad Hafiz Alias said the report would be filed this afternoon, accompanied by documents linked to claims raised in the state legislative assembly and circulated in public discourse.
He said the move was necessary following what he described as serious concerns involving public funds.
"We are taking this seriously as it involves zakat funds in Penang," he told newsmen at the sidelines of the state legislative assembly sitting here today.
Hafiz said the report would focus on several issues, including allegations of direct appointments without interview processes and claims that recruitment within Zakat MAINPP (Penang Islamic Religious Council) did not comply with eligibility requirements.
He said there were also assertions that certain appointments allegedly did not meet age and experience criteria, as well as concerns over governance procedures within the system overseen by MAINPP.
"There is also an alleged restructuring involving zakat-related funds, where a financial arrangement initially recorded as a loan was later converted into a grant following the MAINPP decision.
"This involves significant sums, in the tune of RM16 million, and raises questions over accountability and compliance with proper financial procedures," he added.
Hafiz further alleged that zakat funds were linked to transactions involving a personal bank account during an official overseas delegation, a claim which he said required investigation.
"These are issues that need to be investigated because zakat funds must be managed strictly according to asnaf requirements," he said.
He said the report would include supporting documents and references to MAINPP meeting decisions, including one in 2025, said to have approved changes to the status of zakat-related funds.
He also questioned governance structures and decision-making processes, alleging possible conflicts of interest in the administration of religious and zakat funds in the state.
"We want the MACC to review all documentation submitted and determine whether further investigation is required," he said.
Yesterday, the Penang assembly turned heated after Pas' Penaga assemblyman Mohd Yusni Mat Piah alleged abuse of power, weak governance and poor transparency in agencies linked to MAINPP.
The opposition lawmaker questioned a RM1 million allocation to a company, stressing that
zakat funds
must strictly benefit the eight asnaf groups recognised under Islamic law.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tengku Hassanal reaches Mount Kinabalu summit
Article:
KUNDASANG: Tengku Mahkota of Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah reached the summit of Mount Kinabalu at 8.45am today.
Tengku Hassanal and his entourage began their final ascent at 3.07am after arriving at Panalaban at 3.45pm yesterday.
Sabah Parks assistant park manager (Rescue and Crisis Unit) Julaimin Kamin said His Royal Highness arrived at the Sayat-Sayat checkpoint at 5.05am before continuing the climb at 6.37am and reaching the peak two hours later.
Julaimin said weather conditions were ideal, with temperatures at 9°C, ensuring a safe and smooth expedition.
He said the prince and his entourage spent almost an hour at the summit before beginning their descent to Panalaban at 9.30am.
The climb was part of the three-day Mahkota Borneo Expedition, which concluded today.
The expedition began at Timpohon Gate at 8.05am yesterday before the group reached their accommodation in Panalaban later that afternoon.
Mount Kinabalu, standing at 4,095.2m above sea level, is the highest mountain in Malaysia and is located within the 754sq km Kinabalu Park.
Gazetted in 1964, Kinabalu Park was the country's first Unesco World Heritage Site and is a key biodiversity conservation area.
It is now recognised as the core of the Kinabalu Unesco Global Geopark, which spans 4,750sq km across Kota Belud, Kota Marudu, and parts of Ranau.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sarawak plans to gazette Three Sisters waterfall as protected area
Article:
KUCHING: The Sarawak Forestry Corporation (SFC) is preparing a proposal to gazette Keluan Hill National Park in Baram as a protected area, the state assembly was told today.
Deputy Minister for Urban Planning, Land Administration and Environment Datuk Len Talif Salleh said the proposed area covers about 1,619ha and includes the Three Sisters Waterfall.
He said the site involves eight villages and has undergone several assessment exercises throughout 2025 to evaluate its biodiversity.
"Initial findings recorded no fewer than 66 species of fauna and 142 species of flora, indicating that the area is rich in biodiversity," he said when replying to a question from Datuk Dennis Ngau (GPS-Telang Usan).
Len Talif said the land status is currently being verified, with the gazettement proposal expected to be completed by the third quarter of 2026 once boundaries are finalised.
A development plan for the area will be prepared after the gazettement process is finished.
The gazettement of the area as a Totally Protected Area will bring benefits in terms of well-managed and sustainable governance, protection of natural resources, and biodiversity conservation, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MetMalaysia warns of afternoon rough weather as thunderstorms sweep across parts of peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting eight states until 5pm today.
In a statement today, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather conditions are expected in Perlis, Kedah (Langkawi) and Perak, involving Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim.
Similar conditions are also forecast in Pahang, specifically Cameron Highlands and Bentong, as well as Hulu Selangor in Selangor.
In Johor, the affected areas are Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru.
Meanwhile, in Sabah, the warning covers the Interior areas of Keningau and Tambunan, the West Coast, Tawau, Kinabatangan in Sandakan, and Kudat.
In Sarawak, the warning has been issued for Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Betong, Sarikei, Sibu, Mukah, Song and Kapit in Kapit division, as well as Tatau in Bintulu division. — Bernama

Title: CCTV, phone videos lead to jail for repo man behind Johor Bahru paint-splash attacks
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 13 — A 28-year-old car repossessor was sentenced to a total of seven months in jail by two magistrates today after pleading guilty to splashing red paint on two houses in the city in separate incidents last year and in April.
Muhamad Yaccob admitted to splashing red paint on a house in Taman Mount Austin on April 7 this year and on a condominium unit in Twin Danga Residensi on December 24 last year.
He was sentenced to seven months’ jail for the Taman Mount Austin offence, while a separate magistrates’ court handed him four months’ jail for the Twin Danga Residensi incident, with the sentence ordered to run from his arrest on April 16.
Court documents showed CCTV footage captured Muhamad Yaccob arriving at the homes before splashing paint and fleeing.
Police also found bottles of red paint and videos of the attacks on his mobile phone after inspecting the rental car he was driving.
Lawyers representing the accused appealed for leniency, saying he was married with two children and earned about RM1,000 a month.
However, prosecutors urged the court to impose a deterrent sentence.
Last month, Johor police said six suspects linked to an illegal money-lending syndicate were arrested over criminal intimidation cases involving paint splashing and Molotov cocktail attacks in several states.

Title: No alcohol, please: Wine consumption slides in 2025 due to tariffs, fewer younger consumers
Article:
PARIS, May 13 — Wine consumption fell worldwide last year, the industry’s trade body said Tuesday, amid changing lifestyles and economic pressure on consumers.
The International Organisation of Vine and Wine (OIV) said in its annual review that the industry was confronted in 2025 by “a combination of climatic variability, softer demand and rising trade uncertainty”.
It found consumption slid by 2.7 per cent last year to 208 million hectolitres, taking the cumulative drop since 2018 to 14 per cent.
“This evolution reflects the interaction between longer-term changes in consumption patterns and a more difficult economic environment in recent years,” said the OIV.
“Evolving lifestyle preferences, shifting social habits and generational changes continue to influence consumer behaviour” in several mature wine markets, it added.
The OIV also noted that the wine industry “has faced a succession of external pressures since 2020, including the Covid-19 pandemic, geopolitical tensions, trade disruptions and inflationary pressures, all of which have weighed on purchasing power and consumer confidence”.
It noted that nine of the world’s top 10 wine markets recorded lower volumes, and said three markets played an outsized role in the global decline: China, France and the United States.
In the United States, the world’s leading wine market, consumption fell 4.3 per cent last year, which the OIV put down to reduced purchasing power, a reduction in alcohol consumption among younger consumers and a wider selection of alcoholic beverages.
The impact of tariffs imposed by US President Donald Trump is still difficult to isolate among all of those factors, as well as the drop in the value of the US dollar against the euro, OIV’s director John Barker told AFP.
He added that it was too early to say what the effects of the Middle East war would be, but acknowledged logistical problems and inflation would “all have eventually some sort of knock-on effects on the wine market”.
In France, Europe’s biggest market, consumption fell 3.2 per cent.
In China, wine consumption fell by 13 per cent last year and by 61 per cent since 2020. The OIV said wine demand was particularly sensitive to income and price developments in the country.
OIV’s Barker said economic factors were very important, with inflation boosting production costs and hitting consumers in the pocket.
“Overall you know wine is a discretionary product and people’s purchasing power has been impacted,” he said.
Meanwhile wine production rose 0.6 per cent to 227 million hectolitres in 2025, but the OIV noted this was from a historically low level in 2024.
Need to innovate
It said the third consecutive year of low global output reflected the “combined effect of climatic volatility and production adjustments linked to softer demand conditions”.
OIV’s Barker said the industry is confronted by the need to switch from a volume-driven model to one based on value that puts even more emphasis on the so-called premium-isation of wine—emphasising its superior quality and exclusivity.
Ananda Roy, vice president at US market research firm Circana, said that the wine industry needs to adapt to changing trends and tastes, such as by offering more no and low-alcohol wines (NoLo), smaller bottles and high-quality bag-in-box wine.
Roy said he is “optimistic the sector will seize the moment and innovate beyond just the label and shape of the bottle”.
Barker estimated that NoLo wines currently hold one to two per cent of the global market.
“I think that the technology and the understanding of the product is already developing very very quickly,” he said. — AFP

Title: Serial molester who targeted women on LRT, MRT gets longer jail term, caning despite plea to return to Bangladesh
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — A foreign construction worker was sentenced to 18 months in prison and one stroke of the cane by the High Court here today for outraging the modesty of five women near LRT and MRT stations in the capital last month.
Justice Datuk Aslam Zainuddin imposed the sentence on Md Romjan Ali, a 41-year-old Bangladeshi national, after reviewing the May 6 decisions of four Magistrates’ Courts, which had sentenced him to six months’ imprisonment and RM14,500 fine.
“For all five cases, the court will impose a global sentence. The sentences imposed by the Magistrates’ Courts are set aside and replaced with 18 months’ imprisonment from the date of the accused’s arrest on April 27. For one of the cases, the accused is also ordered to be caned once,” the judge said.
On May 6, Md Romjan pleaded guilty to using criminal force with intent to outrage the modesty of a German woman and four local women aged between 21 and 28.
The offences were committed on footpaths at the Chan Sow Lin LRT Station, inside trains, on the Maluri MRT Station escalator, and inside a train from Taman Midah MRT Station to Muzium Negara MRT Station, between 7.00 am and 10.30 pm from April 10 to 25.
Under Section 354 of the Penal Code, the offence carries a maximum penalty of ten years’ imprisonment, a fine, caning, or any combination thereof.
Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Faridah Nurdinie Bahrum urged the court to impose a deterrent sentence, stressing that it must signal to the public that the court treats outraging modesty with the utmost gravity.
“Committing such offences in public places is unacceptable. Hugging, touching and groping the victims are not normal conduct; no reasonable person would act this way.
“Notably, the accused committed these acts at different times and locations, even when LRT and MRT stations were crowded with commuters,” she said. She further requested an order to deport Md Romjan after he serves his sentence.
Md Romjan’s lawyer, Datuk Dr Mohd Radzuan Ibrahim, applied for a court order to repatriate his client to Bangladesh, stating that family members are willing to cover all arrangements.
“His brother, present in court today, confirms that he (the accused) is suffering emotional distress, and the family is in the process of sending him home for medical treatment. We respectfully request that the court exercise its power to repatriate him.
“As for the fine, we request that it be substituted with a concurrent custodial sentence, as the accused cannot pay. For the court’s information, his work permit is valid until February 2027,” he said. — Bernama

Title: New MACC chief Abdul Halim starts first day in office as Azam Baki exits after 42 years
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman officially began his duties today as the new chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC).
He succeeds Azam Baki, who ended his service yesterday after 42 years with the anti-graft agency.
In a Facebook post, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar also congratulated Abdul Halim on his appointment.
“I believe his credibility and experience in the legal field will continue to add value, strengthen the role of the MACC and elevate its standing as a prestigious enforcement agency both domestically and internationally,” he said.
Shamsul Azri also shared photographs of Abdul Halim receiving his appointment letter as the new MACC chief commissioner.
At the same time, Shamsul Azri also expressed appreciation to Azam for his four decades of service with the commission.
“Many achievements and reforms were implemented under his leadership in steering the MACC as a leading enforcement body.
“Tan Sri Azam’s contributions will always be remembered, and I hope he will continue contributing to the country in whatever capacity in the future,” he said.
On April 25, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, consented to the appointment of Abdul Halim, a former High Court judge, as the new MACC chief commissioner on a two-year contract effective today until May 12, 2028.
Meanwhile, the MACC, in a statement, extended their best wishes to Abdul Halim as he officially reported for duty as the agency’s new chief commissioner.
“The entire MACC workforce extends its congratulations and wishes him success in leading efforts to strengthen integrity, governance and the nation’s anti-corruption agenda,” the statement read. — Bernama

Title: Gua Musang Form One student dies after collapsing at hostel
Article:
KOTA BHARU, May 13 — A male Form One student died after reportedly being found unconscious at the hostel of Sekolah Menengah Paloh in Gua Musang yesterday.
Gua Musang police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said police received a report regarding the incident from the victim’s father at 8.53 am yesterday.
He said the school had taken the 13-year-old boy to Chiku 03 Health Clinic.
“However, medical officers at the clinic confirmed that the victim had died.
“Following that, a post-mortem examination was conducted at Gua Musang Hospital at 3.30 pm on the same day. Preliminary investigations found that the cause of death was due to spontaneous bleeding in the head and fluid in the lungs,” he said in a statement today.
Sik added that further police examination found no elements of crime in the incident.
He said the victim’s body had been handed over to family members for burial arrangements and the case had been classified as sudden death, with further investigations still ongoing.
In this regard, Sik advised the public not to make any speculation regarding the incident as it could affect the sensitivity of the victim’s family. — Bernama

Title: Three more bodies found in Pulau Pangkor migrant boat tragedy
Article:
LUMUT, May 13 — Three more bodies of women, believed to be victims of a boat capsize incident involving undocumented Indonesian migrants off Pulau Pangkor on Monday, have been found.
Marine Police Region One Operations Command officer DSP Mazre Che Mahmod said the three bodies were discovered by Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) vessel KD Sri Indera Sakti about 20 nautical miles from the location of the incident at around 6 pm yesterday.
“Investigations to determine the identities of the three victims are ongoing,” he said when contacted by Bernama today.
Mazre said the latest discovery brought the total number of victims found so far to 30, after 23 of them were rescued on the first day of the search and rescue (SAR) operation on May 11.
“We are still searching for seven more victims. The third day of the SAR operation resumed at 7 am today, involving the Marine Police, RMN and the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA),” he said.
Earlier yesterday, four bodies were found in the waters off Pulau Pangkor.
Today’s SAR operation involves two Marine Police boats, three RMN vessels and three MMEA assets — Maritime Vessel (KM) Malawali, BENTENG 1203 and a Bombardier CL415MP aircraft from the Subang Air Station.
Yesterday, MMEA Perak rescued 23 Indonesian migrants after the boat carrying them capsized in the waters off Pulau Pangkor near Lumut on Monday.
The boat was reported to be carrying 37 undocumented migrants from Indonesia, who were believed to have departed from Kisaran, Indonesia, on May 9 for Malaysia, — Bernama

Title: Maritime industry leader warns bill of lading mismatches can expose shipping sector to major risks
Article:
